mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, lifting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a directional accuracy above 58%, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing GDP growth during expansion phases signals robust manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, driving short-term equity rallies as supply-side fundamentals improve.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than market baseline, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in mid-cycle expansion windows.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation exceeding 2.5 percent triggers market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation triggered instances, or the negative predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth coinciding with declining unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to yield a directional accuracy above 60% over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and driving sustained positive returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth show no statistically significant difference from negative growth periods, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining for two consecutive months signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side fundamentals, leading to downgraded earnings forecasts and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the negative predictive accuracy falls below 55% in out-of-sample testing.
